scenario: A team needs to invoke a legacy desktop application (no API, only GUI) as part of a Camunda 8 process -- open Excel, fill a template, save and email it. Standard Connectors can't do this; the GUI must be driven.
question: Which Camunda 8 feature is purpose-built for this scenario?

options.text: RPA (Robotic Process Automation) -- Camunda 8 has a standalone RPA worker (Robot Framework-based) that drives UI automation (mouse clicks, keyboard input, GUI manipulation). md:59), then reference the deployed RPA script ID in the task properties. md:50-54).

Build a Custom Job Worker that wraps Selenium WebDriver or Robot Framework to drive the Excel GUI programmatically -- the worker subscribes to jobs, launches the browser or desktop automation framework, performs the GUI interactions, and completes the job when finished. While this technically works, it requires significant implementation effort to build and maintain the automation framework integration, bypasses Camunda's native RPA management capabilities, and lacks the deployment and monitoring features that the RPA feature provides.

Use the AI Agent Connector -- configure the LLM with a system prompt describing the Excel automation task and let the LLM's reasoning capability drive the desktop application through API calls or tool invocations. AI Agent Connector is designed for LLM-driven agentic reasoning and decision-making using structured APIs and tool definitions. It has no capability to interact with desktop GUIs, mouse clicks, or keyboard input on a local machine. GUI automation requires a dedicated RPA solution that can drive operating system-level UI interactions.

Add a Manual User Task to the BPMN with detailed instructions for the human operator to manually open Excel, fill the template, save the file, and send the email -- then complete the task in Tasklist to signal process continuation. This completely defeats the automation objective stated in the scenario. The team chose Camunda 8 specifically to automate this process and eliminate manual human intervention. A manual User Task is equivalent to not automating the step at all, incurring human effort, variability, and delay for every process instance.

a.text: Correct. Camunda 8 RPA is the purpose-built feature for UI automation of legacy systems. A Robot Framework script is deployed to the Camunda runtime; the BPMN task type is the RPA connector (not a plain Service Task with an invented camunda::rpa type string). The RPA worker subscribes to those jobs via a configurable label (default default; use a custom label for worker affinity). The bot performs GUI actions; process flow continues when the worker completes the job.

b.text: Incorrect. Custom Job Worker with Selenium can do this but reinvents what RPA provides natively.

c.text: Incorrect. AI Agent Connector is for LLM-driven decisions, not GUI manipulation.

d.text: Incorrect. Manual User Task is the opposite of automation.

explanation: Camunda 8 RPA is the purpose-built feature for UI automation of legacy systems. A Robot Framework script is deployed to the Camunda runtime; the BPMN task type is the RPA connector (not a plain Service Task with an invented camunda::rpa type string). The RPA worker subscribes to those jobs via a configurable label (default default; use a custom label for worker affinity). The bot performs GUI actions; process flow continues when the worker completes the job.
